title: The Homer Of Seville

jokes.text: This concludes our service. Go now in the peace of the Lord. So long, suckers!
jokes.setup: Traditional formal church dismissal blessing
jokes.punchline: Immediately switches to insulting the congregation
jokes.explanation: Sharp juxtaposition between sacred and profane, perfectly economical delivery

jokes.text: Move it, move it, move it! Do you want to be second out of the parking lot? Do you?
jokes.setup: Church service just ended, people leaving peacefully
jokes.punchline: Aggressive militaristic urgency about parking lot order
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of post-church competitive behavior, relatable observation

jokes.text: Leave her! We left her last week, and she didn't make it home for dinner!
jokes.setup: Someone suggesting leaving a person behind
jokes.punchline: Reveals they've done this before with consequences
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation works well, implies ongoing abandonment pattern

jokes.text: You'll never catch me, Lord!
jokes.setup: Just left church service
jokes.punchline: Homer treats God like he's being chased by police
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - treating divine authority like earthly authority

jokes.text: Dad, all you circled were the 'I's and 'A's. Those are words.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming he did Find-A-Word puzzles
jokes.punchline: Only circled single letters, defends them as words
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - technically correct but missing the point entirely

jokes.text: Griddler on the Roof? Thank God It's Fried Eggs? Luftwaffle's? Bodacious Frittatas? Buffet the Hunger Slayer?
jokes.setup: Family discussing brunch restaurants
jokes.punchline: Series of pun-based restaurant names mixing food with pop culture
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay series, particularly 'Buffet the Hunger Slayer' is inspired

jokes.text: I want to say one. Be my guest. Denny's.
jokes.setup: After elaborate punny restaurant names, Bart wants to suggest one
jokes.punchline: Just says the most generic chain restaurant
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation after elaborate setup, brilliant economy and misdirection

jokes.text: I just want a simple salad with pine nuts, haricots verts, seasonal heirloom tomatoes... Forget it!
jokes.setup: Marge claiming she wants something simple
jokes.punchline: Lists incredibly fancy ingredients, realizes the contradiction
jokes.explanation: Good Marge character moment showing her aspirational vs. practical sides

jokes.text: Your mom is way too classy a lady to crash a private... I'm not cooking! And you can cut your own meat!
jokes.setup: Homer defending Marge's classiness about crashing parties
jokes.punchline: Marge immediately agrees to crash the party
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection and character reversal, Marge's practicality overriding propriety

jokes.text: Everyone just act cool. If anyone asks, tell them we're plumbers, and then start plumbing until they go away.
jokes.setup: Family crashing someone's house, need cover story
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous plan to actually perform plumbing as disguise
jokes.explanation: Absurd Homer logic - overly complex solution with commitment to the bit

jokes.text: Dude! You don't think, you know me, but you do!
jokes.setup: Stranger approaching Homer at party
jokes.punchline: Confusing, overly familiar greeting to complete stranger
jokes.explanation: Mild awkwardness but not particularly clever or memorable

jokes.text: Mmm! Au jus! Not quite gravy, not quite blood.
jokes.setup: Homer trying fancy roast beef au jus
jokes.punchline: Describes it in unappetizing but accurate terms
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer food observation - crude but not wrong, good economy

jokes.text: Oh, I can't wait to taste that hot fudge, and caramel and crushed nuts all slathered over a delicious... elderly corpse?!
jokes.setup: Homer excited about what he thinks is ice cream sundae bar
jokes.punchline: Realizes it's people viewing a corpse at funeral
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect horror/comedy contrast, strong structure

jokes.text: We crashed a funeral! Oh, my God! Quick, everyone, put on their long faces. Longer!
jokes.setup: Family realizes they're at a funeral, not a party
jokes.punchline: Homer directing family to look more appropriately sad
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy direction, family's attempt at instant propriety

jokes.text: Mahogany. Great movie, great casket.
jokes.setup: Bart at funeral viewing casket
jokes.punchline: Reviews casket like a movie, both happen to be mahogany
jokes.explanation: Clever double meaning, perfect deadpan delivery, excellent economy

jokes.text: Pop-pop said that after he died, you should give me ten bucks a week. Starting now. Okay.
jokes.setup: Bart talking to mourner about deceased grandfather
jokes.punchline: Scams money claiming false deathbed request
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart scheming, taking advantage of grief and guilt

jokes.text: Will the tragedies that stalk this family never cease?
jokes.setup: Person mentions brother twisted ankle setting up chairs
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic response to minor injury at already tragic event
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic overstatement, plays on funeral melodrama

jokes.text: I thought she said polar bear.
jokes.setup: Asked to be a pallbearer at funeral
jokes.punchline: Homer misheard and thought it was something about polar bears
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer mishearing, good character consistency and economy

jokes.text: Oh, my back! It hurts, and not in a good way.
jokes.setup: Homer hurt his back carrying casket
jokes.punchline: Specifies it's not the good kind of back pain
jokes.explanation: Mildly amusing Homer observation, implies there's usually good back pain

jokes.text: Well, you're always telling me I should eat more dirt. Not dirt. Vegetables! Which grow in what?
jokes.setup: Doctor concerned Homer ingested grave dirt
jokes.punchline: Homer uses twisted logic to justify eating dirt
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic loop, technically correct but missing the point

jokes.text: That doesn't sound like Homer's usual annoyed grunt. D'oh! D'oh. Fascinating.
jokes.setup: Homer's back injury changes his voice
jokes.punchline: Doctor notices his signature grunt sounds different
jokes.explanation: Nice meta reference to Homer's catchphrase, sets up singing plot

jokes.text: When Homer's on his back, his stomach lodges perfectly under his diaphragm, giving him a powerful singing voice. Is that a real thing? It is!
jokes.setup: Doctor explains medical reason for Homer's singing
jokes.punchline: Lisa breaks fourth wall asking if it's real, doctor confirms
jokes.explanation: Lampshades absurd premise, meta moment with Lisa as audience surrogate

jokes.text: Let's see, I'll take his liver, a case of Adam's apples, that motorcycle man's moustache.
jokes.setup: Mr. Burns shopping in what appears to be organ harvesting facility
jokes.punchline: Casually ordering body parts like groceries
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns character moment - casual evil and body harvesting

jokes.text: Oh, the money you've contributed to anti-helmet laws has really paid off, sir.
jokes.setup: Context of Burns harvesting motorcycle accident victim parts
jokes.punchline: Reveals Burns funded anti-safety legislation for profit
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy revealing Burns' systematic evil and profit motive

jokes.text: Well, young people are my future.
jokes.setup: Burns explaining his investment in anti-helmet laws
jokes.punchline: Perverts caring phrase to justify harvesting young people's organs
jokes.explanation: Masterful dark irony - wholesome sentiment twisted into sinister meaning

jokes.text: Oh, oh, I just have one question: what is this movie about?
jokes.setup: Homer about to perform in opera La Bohème
jokes.punchline: Still thinks it's a movie and doesn't know the plot
jokes.explanation: Good Homer obliviousness, but fairly predictable character beat

jokes.text: For the last time, it's not a movie! Excuse me, 'film.'
jokes.setup: Director frustrated with Homer's movie confusion
jokes.punchline: Homer 'corrects' himself with even more wrong terminology
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer thinks he's being more sophisticated

jokes.text: Homer's fantastic! Yeah. But these seats are terrible.
jokes.setup: Family watching Homer's opera debut
jokes.punchline: Complains about seating during proud moment
jokes.explanation: Mild deflation of proud moment with practical complaint

jokes.text: This makes up for me showing up drunk to the father-daughter dance. The dance isn't till next week. Sorry, Lisa. Can't change the future.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to make amends with Lisa
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's pre-planned to show up drunk to future event
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - fatalistic about his own bad behavior, treats it as inevitable

jokes.text: Wow, praise from Placido Domingo. Just call me P-Dingo. Eh, I'll think about it.
jokes.setup: Famous opera singer offers casual nickname
jokes.punchline: Homer noncommittally considers the friendly gesture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer social obliviousness - treating celebrity friendship casually

jokes.text: You know, of the three tenors, you're my second favorite! No wait, I forgot about that other guy. Sorry, you're third.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to compliment Placido Domingo
jokes.punchline: Accidentally demotes him while trying to be nice
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Homer's attempt at praise backfires through honesty

jokes.text: But I need my childhood friends to help me keep it real. Would you have me keep it fake?
jokes.setup: Marge wants romantic dinner without Homer's entourage
jokes.punchline: Homer uses twisted logic to justify bringing friends
jokes.explanation: Homer misapplies celebrity culture logic to romantic dinner

jokes.text: We'll move to another table. Yoko.
jokes.setup: Friends leaving Homer and Marge alone
jokes.punchline: Barney calls Marge 'Yoko' like she's breaking up the band
jokes.explanation: Perfect pop culture reference applied to domestic situation, great economy

jokes.text: To the hottest girls in the room.
jokes.setup: Homer signing autographs for fans
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate flirtation while wife watches
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer insensitivity, sets up marital tension but not particularly clever

jokes.text: I said you look hot! Ay Chihuahua! Ay chi-what? Hua-hua!
jokes.setup: Homer flirting with female fan
jokes.punchline: Fan's confusion leading to breakdown of Spanish exclamation
jokes.explanation: Weak wordplay that doesn't serve character or story, feels forced

jokes.text: May I say I'm enjoying the calm waters of your deep, blue eyes?
jokes.setup: Male fan compliments Homer's performance
jokes.punchline: Homer responds with romantic poetry to another man
jokes.explanation: Homer's oblivious inappropriate flirting extended to men, mild awkwardness

jokes.text: That's how guys talk.
jokes.setup: Marge questioning Homer's romantic language to male fan
jokes.punchline: Homer defends obviously romantic language as normal male interaction
jokes.explanation: Good Homer obliviousness and defensive logic, economical delivery

jokes.text: You've actually outgrown your cape!
jokes.setup: Marge listing Homer's out-of-control behavior
jokes.punchline: Physical evidence of his excess literally outgrowing costume
jokes.explanation: Visual gag about Homer's weight gain, fairly straightforward

jokes.text: I really want to hook up with Homer. Now we both know that ain't gonna happen, but, uh, I'm right here.
jokes.setup: Barney offering to take groupies shopping
jokes.punchline: Directly propositioning them as consolation prize
jokes.explanation: Good Barney character moment - pathetic but self-aware pickup attempt

jokes.text: A lady motorcycle driver?! What is this, The Twilight Zone?
jokes.setup: Female motorcycle rider rescues Homer and Marge
jokes.punchline: Homer's outdated gender assumptions treated as supernatural
jokes.explanation: Good Homer sexism played for laughs, references classic TV show

jokes.text: Switching to thermal imaging. Target acquired.
jokes.setup: Fans tracking Homer with opera glasses
jokes.punchline: Military-grade surveillance technology for celebrity stalking
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of fan obsession but feels like generic celebrity satire

jokes.text: Remember the part where I forgot the words and I just sang 'Uh-oh Spaghetti-os'? I'm hoping they send me a case.
jokes.setup: Homer discussing his opera performance
jokes.punchline: Admits to singing commercial jingle, hopes for sponsorship
jokes.explanation: Good Homer materialism and opportunism, memorable phrase

jokes.text: Um... everyone's wearing clothes in here.
jokes.setup: Julia trying to seduce Homer, Marge asking what's wrong
jokes.punchline: Homer's awkward deflection about obvious fact
jokes.explanation: Homer's panic creating absurd excuse, mild cringe comedy

jokes.text: This opera's over when the fat man sings.
jokes.setup: Homer rejecting Julia's advances
jokes.punchline: Plays on 'it's not over till the fat lady sings' with gender swap
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay subverting famous phrase, perfect self-reference and context

jokes.text: Before you go, could you hire your own replacement? But make sure she's not crazy like you.
jokes.setup: Julia leaving after rejection
jokes.punchline: Homer asks stalker to find her own replacement, insulting her
jokes.explanation: Homer's oblivious insensitivity, asking rejected stalker for favor while insulting her

jokes.text: Stu, stu, stu, stu-pid Flanders.
jokes.setup: Homer practicing vocal exercises
jokes.punchline: Uses neighbor's name in insulting vocal exercise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer-Flanders dynamic, incorporating insult into vocal practice

jokes.text: Get lost you waste of a moustache. Okily-dokily.
jokes.setup: Homer insulting Flanders
jokes.punchline: Flanders responds with cheerful catchphrase despite insult
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer-Flanders interaction, Flanders' unflappable niceness

jokes.text: It ain't candy, it's a lamb chop wrapped in foil.
jokes.setup: Someone complaining about loud candy unwrapping at opera
jokes.punchline: Reveals person is eating full meal wrapped in foil
jokes.explanation: Good unexpected escalation from candy to full meal at opera

jokes.text: Luckily, the sniper bullets stopped her heart from pumping the poison through her body long enough for the chandelier to squish it all out of her.
jokes.setup: Explaining Julia's medical condition after multiple attacks
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous medical explanation involving bullets, poison, and chandelier
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist medical explanation combining all attack methods

jokes.text: And I scheduled the cable guy to come on Wednesday between 10:00 and 2:00. Oh, but Wednesday's not good for me. I know, Marge. I know.
jokes.setup: Julia threatening final revenge on Marge
jokes.punchline: Threatens her with cable installation appointment inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Brilliant anticlimatic revenge - ultimate domestic inconvenience as final threat

jokes.text: Let's go, Hagliacci, or should I say, Madame Butterface?
jokes.setup: Chief Wiggum arresting Julia
jokes.punchline: Mangles opera titles with insults (Pagliacci/Madame Butterfly)
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum malapropism combining opera references with insults

jokes.text: What? What? I had two.
jokes.setup: Wiggum confused by reaction to his opera puns
jokes.punchline: Proudly defends making two puns like it's an achievement
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum character moment - proud of his bad wordplay

jokes.text: I'm retiring from the opera. It's just too popular.
jokes.setup: Homer ending his opera career after dramatic events
jokes.punchline: Treating popularity as a problem rather than violence/stalking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer missing the point about why opera career was problematic

jokes.text: Singing opera made me good at painting. Is that a real thing? No.
jokes.setup: Homer claims opera singing improved his painting
jokes.punchline: Lisa asks if it's real, gets honest 'no'
jokes.explanation: Callbacks the earlier 'Is that a real thing?' meta moment, but weaker

tmdb_overview: A freak accident gives Homer an operatic singing voice, and he becomes a star at the Springfield Opera. But with fame and fortune comes a price: He's continually bothered by fans, an obsessed woman in particular.
